exemplar.title:
Title: Gendered bias within sports design and how this 
disadvantages female athletes. 
Research question: Between boot design, physiological differences, 
and poor training and pitch quality - which factor plays the most 
detrimental role in the high ACL injury rates among female 
footballers? (With reference to the Arsenal womenâs team in the 
2022-23 season) 
Subjects: World Studies (DT and SEHS) 
Theme: Equality and Inequality

detailedExemplarData.title:
Title: Gendered bias within sports design and how this 
disadvantages female athletes. 
Research question: Between boot design, physiological differences, 
and poor training and pitch quality - which factor plays the most 
detrimental role in the high ACL injury rates among female 
footballers? (With reference to the Arsenal womenâs team in the 
2022-23 season) 
Subjects: World Studies (DT and SEHS) 
Theme: Equality and Inequality

strengths: The research topic and global/local manifestations are articulated clearly and consistently throughout, maintaining strong focus.
The research question is sharply phrased around three bounded variables and remains explicit and well-anchored to the case study.
Methodology selection is informed and clearly linked to both SEHS and DT strands, demonstrating thoughtful source and method choice.
weaknesses: Repeated restatement of the full research question disrupts flow and could be streamlined.
Key procedural details (frame-selection criteria, calibration, inter-rater reliability) are insufficiently specified.
Occasional anecdotal framing sentences deviate slightly from analytical focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place topic communication and question formulation in the 5â6 band and methodology in the 3â4 band. Overall performance clearly meets upper-band descriptors with only minor diffusion in scope and some methodological detail gaps, justifying a score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Terminology and concepts from both SEHS and DT are used accurately, consistently, and with depth.
Source material is generally relevant, including peer-reviewed studies and industry reports that support key claims.
Knowledge is coherently integrated, linking anatomical, biomechanical, and economic insights to the research question.
weaknesses: Several non-academic and less authoritative sources (e.g., Wikipedia, tabloids) reduce overall source rigor.
Some evidence is presented descriptively without deeper statistical interrogation of quantitative data.
A few citations are incomplete or duplicated, indicating minor lapses in academic rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source relevance and knowledge demonstration lie solidly in the 3â4 band, while use of discipline-specific terminology reaches the 5â6 band. The work exhibits coherent integration and strong conceptual command with only occasional lapses, warranting an overall score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: The argument is interdisciplinary, logically sequenced, and maintains coherence from evidence to conclusion.
The student acknowledges alternative viewpoints and limitations, demonstrating nuanced evaluation.
Critical insights, such as reframing physiological differences as design challenges, show mature synthesis.
weaknesses: Primary data analysis lacks statistical depth (no confidence intervals or broader sampling).
Some causal links are asserted rather than empirically demonstrated, indicating inferential leaps.
Reliance on small sample sizes and a few speculative claims (e.g., pitch inequality resolving over time) limit full rigour.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality and analysis strands meet the 4â6 band, while argument structure clearly reaches the 10â12 band. Balancing the highâlevel critical synthesis against methodological and inferential limitations places the overall Critical Thinking score at 10 out of 12.

strengths: The report follows a clear academic structure with coherent headings, pagination, and a well-organized table of contents.
Visual elements (frame sequences, tables, measurement photos) generally support the analysis and are captioned.
A complete bibliography and in-text citations are present, fulfilling core academic requirements.
weaknesses: Citation style fluctuates between formats, and some in-text references lack details (page numbers, dates).
Several images are low resolution or duplicated, and figure numbering restarts inconsistently.
OCR line breaks and formatting errors interrupt sentence flow and readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Academic structure and visuals are solidly in the mid-band (3â4), while referencing falls in the lower band (1â2). The overall Presentation performance averages to a score of 3 out of 4, reflecting consistent but imperfect formatting and visual support.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The essay develops a logically sequenced, interdisciplinary argument that integrates SEHS biomechanics with DT design economics, repeatedly relating evidence back to the central question.  Each factor is critiqued, alternative viewpoints are acknowledged, and limitations are discussed, evidencing nuanced evaluation.  Transitions between sections are smooth, sustaining a coherent line of reasoning that culminates in a justified conclusion.

Some inferential leaps and the modest statistical depth prevent the highest possible score within the 10â12 band, but the structure and critical synthesis clearly exceed the 7â9 level.  A score of 10 is therefore warranted.

A complete bibliography and in-text citations are present, fulfilling basic academic requirements.  Word count, title and pagination are provided as checklist elements.

However, citation style fluctuates between Harvard, APA, bare URLs and duplicates appear.  Some in-text references lack page numbers or dates, and figure referencing is inconsistent.  Consequently, referencing is present but not consistently formatted, matching the 1-2 band with a score of 2.

The student states the global issue, local manifestation, title and research focus explicitly in the opening section and reiterates them throughout the essay.  The connection between gender bias in design and ACL injury in womenâs football is made explicit, allowing the reader to grasp the scope and significance of the topic from the outset.  

Minor weaknesses remain: the repeated restatement of the full research question within headings occasionally disrupts flow, and some framing sentences are anecdotal rather than analytical.  Nonetheless, the topic is communicated clearly and remains visible across the essay, meeting the descriptors for the 5â6 band.

The report follows a recognisable academic format with title page, abstract elements, contents page, numbered headings and sub-headings.  Pagination is consistent and sections flow logically.  

Nonetheless, occasional repetition of headers, some incomplete sentences, and the resetting of figure numbers detract from flawless structure.  These issues keep the work within, but not at the top of, the 3-4 band, meriting a score of 3.

Across sections the student demonstrates accurate understanding of ACL anatomy, biomechanics, gendered foot morphology, and design economics.  Explanations are generally clear, and concepts are applied to the case study with reasonable effectiveness.

Yet some areas remain descriptive (e.g., historical background) and quantitative data are presented without deeper statistical interrogation.  The essay occasionally asserts causation from limited evidence.  Overall, knowledge is sound and mostly applied appropriately, meriting the upper level of the 3-4 band.

The student conducts detailed frame-by-frame biomechanical analyses and a comparative boot measurement study, then triangulates these with literature to reach a clear, reasoned conclusion that boot design is the dominant factor.  Counter-arguments (anatomy, pitch/training) are considered and weighed, showing some evaluation.

However, depth of quantitative analysis is limited: no statistical testing, confidence intervals, or broader datasets are used, and some causal links are asserted rather than demonstrated.  Conclusions are mostly but not completely supported.  This aligns with the top of the 4-6 band.

The research question is presented verbatim on the title page and in the introduction.  It is sharply phrased around three clearly-bounded variables (boot design, physiology, pitch/training quality) and anchored to a defined case (Arsenal Women 2022-23).  Throughout the body, each section is explicitly linked back to one of these variables, showing that the investigation remains focused.  

The question is ambitious and complex, but it is still sufficiently narrow to be addressed in 4 000 words.  Occasional breadth (e.g., side discussions of historical bans) does not materially blur the focus.  These features align with the upper band; slight diffusion prevents full marks, so a score of 5 is appropriate.

The research base covers biomechanics, design economics, and sociocultural factors, with most sources current and discipline-appropriate.  Primary data, though limited, are relevant to the question, and secondary literature is largely well chosen.  

Gaps remain: small primary samples, sparse use of statistical tests, and reliance on some media sources reduce overall rigour.  Nevertheless, the majority of evidence is pertinent and adequately supports the inquiry, meeting the upper edge of the 4-6 band.

The student explains the choice of SEHS and DT, outlines primary (video frame analysis, boot measurements) and secondary research, and discusses biases and limitations (e.g., small sample sizes, gender data gap).  Methods are clearly linked to strands of each discipline and to the research variables, evidencing informed selection.

However, important procedural details are under-developed: there is no explanation of how frames were selected, how measurements were calibrated, or how reliability/validity was ensured (e.g., inter-rater checks, repeated trials).  Statistical treatment is minimal.  Thus, while the methodology is generally appropriate, it is not fully comprehensive, placing it comfortably in the 3-4 band at the top end.

A wide range of peer-reviewed journals, official reports, and industry documents is consulted, demonstrating clear effort to select material of relevance across both disciplines.  Sources are generally well integrated into the discussion and support key claims.

Nevertheless, the inclusion of several non-academic or less authoritative sources (Wikipedia, tabloid articles) and some duplicated entries indicate that source selection, while mostly appropriate, is not uniformly rigorous.  The performance data and statistical studies that could strengthen quantitative claims are limited in depth.  These factors place the work solidly in the 3-4 band with a score of 4.

Numerous visuals (injury frame sequences, measurement photographs, comparative tables) directly support the analysis and are generally captioned and referenced.  Their inclusion aids comprehension of biomechanical discussion and design comparisons.

Quality, however, is uneven: several images are low-resolution or duplicated, some figure labels are inconsistent, and numbering restarts.  These shortcomings align with mid-band performance, so a score of 3 within the 3-4 band is appropriate.

The essay employs discipline-specific language fluentlyâterms such as âdynamic knee valgus,â âintercondylar notch width,â âneuromuscular perturbation,â and âergonomics/anthropometricsâ are used accurately and consistently.  Concepts from SEHS (biomechanics, hormonal influence) and DT (last design, stud configuration, market analysis) are combined coherently, demonstrating depth of conceptual grasp.

Minor slips (e.g., occasional undefined statistics) do not materially detract from the overall precision.  The work sits comfortably in the 5â6 band; a score of 5 recognises strong but not flawless conceptual command.

annotations.comment: The methodology outlines primary and secondary methods but lacks detail on frame selection criteria, calibration procedures, or interârater reliability checks.
The citation for Williamsonâs injury boots references a popular media source; consider supporting with peerâreviewed literature on boot design to strengthen the discussion.
The student presents a nuanced perspective by reframing physiological differences as factors to be mitigated through design, showing mature critical insight.
The use of largeâscale publication review data (6% femaleâonly vs 31% maleâonly) effectively demonstrates the gender data gap, integrating quantitative evidence.
The historical account of the FA ban, while interesting, deviates from the methodological focus; consider condensing this to maintain strict relevance to the research question.
The examination of pitch allocation practices and coach access vividly illustrates external training inequalities, showing strong knowledge application.
The economic analysis of unisex boot production is compelling, offering clear insight into corporate motivation and market dynamics.
Line breaks caused by OCR interrupt sentence flow; ensure that the final submitted version has continuous, unbroken text for clarity.
The critical analysis clearly links boot fit, stud configuration, and user mass differences to ACL risk, demonstrating integrated knowledge across disciplines.
The discussion of stud configuration impact would benefit from specifying how traction measurements were obtained and any repeatability checks performed.
The student has provided an explicit word count at the start of the report, satisfying the formatting requirement and demonstrating adherence to IB regulations.
The title is clearly stated and matched by the research question, establishing focus from the outset in line with IB formatting criteria.
The table of contents is wellâstructured with coherent headings and page references, facilitating navigation and reflecting clear academic structure.
The repeated placement of the full research question at the top of every page interrupts the readerâs flow; consider moving it to an appendix or header for brevity.
The introduction clearly articulates both the global issue and its local manifestation, demonstrating a wellâdefined research focus from the beginning.
The statement about a "gender bias permeating design" would benefit from a supporting citation to strengthen the claim and link evidence to the global context.
The final sentence in this paragraph is cut off midâthought, indicating a formatting or transcription error; ensure complete sentences for clarity.
The videoâanalysis methodology is underâspecified: the criteria for selecting frames and ensuring measurement validity are not described.
Comparison visuals are helpful, but the analysis lacks quantitative traction data; consider including forceâplate or in situ traction measurements for deeper insight.
The table caption omits a publication date or full source details adjacent to the figure; include full citation in the caption for completeness.
The student demonstrates coherent integration of SEHS and DT methodologies, explicitly linking each disciplineâs methods to facets of the research question.
Primary video analysis is insightful but based on a limited sample with no statistical treatment; consider broader case sampling or confidence intervals to support generalisation.
Several images appear low resolution and lack consistent figure numbering; improve image quality and standardise captions for better visual support.
The frameâbyâframe biomechanical analysis uses precise terminology (e.g., "dynamic knee valgus", "neurocognitive perturbation"), demonstrating detailed critical evaluation.
The claim that pitch and training inequality will resolve over time is speculative; provide longitudinal data or case studies to support this conclusion.
The inclusion of calibration tools (digital calipers and ruler) in figures 3.1 and 3.2 effectively demonstrates attention to measurement accuracy.
The comparison between Mead and Williamsonâs injuries is well founded, effectively triangulating primary analysis with external research to identify common risk factors.
The student applies anatomical data such as quadriceps angle ranges (11â20Â° vs 8â14Â°) accurately, integrating biomechanical concepts coherently to explain increased ACL risk.
An incomplete citation appears at the bottom of the page; ensure all references are fully listed to maintain academic rigor.
The discussion of hormonal influences would benefit from quantitative injuryârate comparisons across menstrual phases to substantiate the proposed correlation.
The argument that pitch and training factors exacerbate but do not originate the ACL epidemic is logically structured with evidence from the local manifestation.
This paragraph is dense and could be broken into subâparagraphs or bullet points to enhance readability and maintain academic flow.
Measurement methodology is not sufficiently detailed: indicate number of trials, statistical error margins, and calibration frequency to strengthen reliability.
The tabulated dimension comparison provides clear quantitative support for differences in boot geometry, directly informing design critique.
The studentâs reflection on research strengths (global scope) and weaknesses (limited primary data) demonstrates thoughtful selfâevaluation and insight.
The bibliography includes multiple referencing styles and duplicate entries; apply a single consistent citation format throughout to meet IB referencing standards.
The bibliography heading is clearly formatted and labeled, aligning with consistent academic presentation guidelines.
